step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("examples of placebo vs sham surgery")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the provided evidence, my position remains consistent with earlier assessments—that **sham surgery** represents a quintessential example of a placebo. Here’s why:
Sham surgery, much like traditional placebos such as sugar pills, operates entirely on the principle of expectation and belief. Patients undergoing sham surgeries report improved outcomes simply because they believe they underwent actual corrective procedures, highlighting the powerful role of psychological factors in perceived wellness. Unlike herbal medications (Option A), physiotherapy (Option B), or cognitive-behavioral therapy (Option D)—which offer either biological components or active interventions targeting symptom relief—sham surgeries rely solely on the power of suggestion and faith in medical expertise.

Based on the provided evidence, my position remains consistent with earlier assessments—that **sham surgery** represents a quintessential example of a placebo. Here’s why:
Sham surgery, much like traditional placebos such as sugar pills, operates entirely on the principle of expectation and belief. Patients undergoing sham surgeries report improved outcomes simply because they believe they underwent actual corrective procedures, highlighting the powerful role of psychological factors in perceived wellness. Unlike herbal medications (Option A), physiotherapy (Option B), or cognitive-behavioral therapy (Option D)—which offer either biological components or active interventions targeting symptom relief—sham surgeries rely solely on the power of suggestion and faith in medical expertise.
Therefore, continuing agreement stands firm: \boxed{\text{C}}
